subject: [PATCH 1/6] mm: add softleaf_to_pmd() and convert existing callers

2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch adds a new function `softleaf_to_pmd()` that generates a PMD entry from a leaf entry, completing the symmetry of the softleaf abstraction. It also converts existing callers of `swp_entry_to_pmd()` to use `softleaf_to_pmd()`, keeping feature patches focused on new functionality rather than refactoring. The patch is part of a series and has been reviewed by David Hildenbrand (Arm) and Zi Yan (Nvidia).

reviews.author: Lance Yang
reviews.summary: Identified no issues with the patch, gave it an LGTM. Suggested adding a Reviewed-by tag.
reviews.raw_body:
On Tue, Jun 30, 2026 at 09:34:38AM -0700, Usama Arif wrote:
>Add softleaf_to_pmd() as the PMD counterpart to softleaf_to_pte(),
>completing the symmetry of the softleaf abstraction for page table
>leaf entries.
>
>The upcoming PMD swap entry support needs to construct PMD entries
>from swap entries. Converting existing swp_entry_to_pmd() callers
>to softleaf_to_pmd() in a prep patch keeps the feature patches
>focused on new functionality rather than mixing refactoring with
>new code.
>
>Acked-by: David Hildenbrand (Arm) <david@kernel.org>
>Reviewed-by: Zi Yan <ziy@nvidia.com>
>Signed-off-by: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>
>---

LGTM. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Lance Yang <lance.yang@linux.dev>

2026-07-03.patch_summary: This patch series adds a new function, softleaf_to_pmd(), which generates a PMD entry from a leaf entry, completing the symmetry of the softleaf abstraction for page table leaf entries. The existing callers of swp_entry_to_pmd() are converted to use softleaf_to_pmd(). This is done in preparation for upcoming PMD swap entry support.
